reason: The finding concerns enabling FIPS mode via a kernel boot flag, which is a bootloader/kernel parameter. Incorrect configuration can persist and cannot be fixed on a running system, requiring a reinstall. This aligns with the too_dangerous_to_remediate category for bootloader/FIPS changes.
estimated_impact: reboot required; FIPS mode not enabled reduces cryptographic assurance; System may fail to meet FIPS 140-2 or other compliance requirements; Incorrect boot configuration can prevent system boot
